chunk_summary: Guidelines for initiating iron therapy during the rehabilitation phase of SAM management, emphasizing clinical stability, dosing, and monitoring criteria.
learning_objectives: Understand the timing and prerequisites for initiating iron therapy in SAM rehabilitation.
Learn the recommended dosage and duration of elemental iron supplementation.
Recognize the role of transfusion criteria in managing severe anemia in SAM patients.
Identify triggers for reassessment in cases of poor weight gain or persistent anemia.
key_facts: Iron supplementation is delayed until the child is clinically stable, typically around day 7 of inpatient care.
Recommended dose of elemental iron is approximately 3 mg/kg/day during rehabilitation.
Severe anemia (Hb <4 g/dL or Hb 4–6 g/dL with respiratory distress) requires cautious blood transfusion.
Iron should not be added if the child is receiving correctly formulated RUTF unless clinically indicated.
Persistent poor weight gain or anemia triggers reassessment for infection, inflammation, parasitosis, or hemoglobinopathy.
reference_quote: Iron is delayed in SAM because early supplementation during stabilization can increase oxidative stress and may worsen outcomes when infection is uncontrolled.
document_key_topics: Iron initiation criteria in SAM rehabilitation
Dosing and monitoring of elemental iron
Management of severe anemia in SAM
